data_sources: City of Miami Beach Official Website (miamibeachfl.gov)
metadata.notes: IMPORTANT: City of Miami (managed by Miami Parking Authority) and City of Miami Beach are SEPARATE municipalities with DIFFERENT rules. Coral Gables is also a separate city. All three enforce independently.

city_of_miami.overview: The City of Miami does NOT operate an aggressive alternate-side street sweeping program like NYC or SF. Street sweeping is handled by the Public Works / Solid Waste Department but does not involve routine ticketing/towing of parked cars on a published schedule. The city contracts private sweeping companies for arterial and commercial streets. Residential areas do not have posted no-parking-for-sweeping signs as a standard practice.

contact.department: City of Miami Solid Waste Department

city_of_miami.enforcement_model: Miami does NOT ticket or tow for street sweeping on residential streets. Sweeping happens around parked cars. This is fundamentally different from cities like SF, LA, or NYC.
city_of_miami.commercial_arterials: Major commercial corridors (Biscayne Blvd, Flagler St, Calle Ocho) are swept by contract crews, typically overnight or early morning when traffic is minimal. No posted no-parking restrictions for sweeping on these roads.

city_of_miami_beach.overview: Miami Beach has a more active street cleaning program, particularly in residential neighborhoods like Flamingo Park and North Beach. However, the program has faced delays and community pushback.

flamingo_park.status: DELAYED - Implementation postponed while city works through resident concerns
flamingo_park.proposed_plan: Alternate side parking restrictions between 9:00 AM and 12:00 PM on specific weekdays
flamingo_park.notes: When implemented, parking would be restricted on one side of the street at a time to allow sweeping equipment access

north_beach.restriction_hours: 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM on designated weekdays
north_beach.model: Alternate side parking - one side of street restricted per designated day
north_beach.notes: Check posted signage on each block for specific day restrictions

commercial_areas.south_beach_entertainment_district: Commercial corridors (Ocean Drive, Collins Ave, Washington Ave) are swept overnight/early morning by city sanitation crews. No specific no-parking restrictions posted for sweeping.
commercial_areas.general: The Sanitation Division handles commercial street cleaning outside of peak hours

city_of_miami_beach.penalties: Vehicles parked in violation of posted street cleaning signs face parking citations. Towing may occur in persistent or egregious cases.

contact.department: City of Miami Beach Public Works - Sanitation Division

city_of_west_miami.note: Separate municipality - has its own published street sweeping calendar at cityofwestmiami.gov

city_of_miami.general_enforcement_hours: Most meters: 8:00 AM to midnight, 7 days/week. Some areas vary (Wynwood: 7 AM - 2 AM).

mobile_apps.name: ParkMobile
mobile_apps.features: Pay by zone number, extend time

payment_methods.notes: Zone numbers posted on signage at each meter location. Must enter correct zone number for enforcement validation.

resident_discount.amount: Up to 50% discount on on-street parking
resident_discount.how: Register as City of Miami resident with Miami Parking Authority; discount applied through ParkMobile/PayByPhone
resident_discount.eligibility: Must be City of Miami resident with proof of residency

downtown_miami.notes: Metered spots along Biscayne Blvd and side streets. Very limited on-street supply. MPA garages offer first 30 min free.

brickell.notes: Very limited metered street parking, mostly along SE 15th St. Most parking is in private garages. Brickell City Centre: first 2 hours free. Mary Brickell Village: 2 hours free with Publix validation.

wynwood.boundaries: NW 20th St to NW 29th St, railroad tracks west to NW 6th Ave
wynwood.notes: Pay By Phone program. Employee parking decals available ($30/month, ~260 spaces). Monthly parking: $55/month (~85 spaces). Private lots in area often charge $4+/hour on weekends/events. Limited metered spots along NW 36th and NE 29th.
wynwood.special_note: Rates on private lots vary dramatically during Art Basel, Wynwood Walls events, and weekend nights. $20-40 flat fees common on private lots during peak times.

coconut_grove.notes: Meters free until 10 AM. MPA-managed public facilities available. Some free customer parking at retail locations (Sushi Maki: 2 hours free).

little_havana.key_street: SW 8th Street (Calle Ocho) between 13th and 18th Avenues
little_havana.free_parking: Lot 82 in Cultural District (free). Adjacent streets SW 5th and SW 7th often have free unrestricted parking.
little_havana.resident_discount: 20% discount for City of Miami residents via Pay By Phone

design_district.payment: Contactless via ParkWhiz app or parkwhiz.com (enter license plate)
design_district.street_meters: Limited MPA metered spots in surrounding streets. MPA manages some on-street meters in the broader Fashion/Design District area.

midtown.notes: Midtown Miami CDD operates its own parking garages (Northblock Garage, Southblock Garage). Street meters managed by MPA.

city_of_miami_beach.managed_by: City of Miami Beach Parking Department
city_of_miami_beach.infrastructure: 66 surface lots, 12 garages, 19 residential permit zones
city_of_miami_beach.technology: Pay-by-plate, multi-space pay stations, pay-on-foot garage systems
city_of_miami_beach.free_parking_window: 3:00 AM to 9:00 AM daily (all on-street meters)
city_of_miami_beach.rate_increase_status: Planned October 2024 rate increase was REVERSED on September 25, 2024 by unanimous commission vote. Rates remain at pre-increase levels.

south_beach.notes: Most expensive zone. Extremely high enforcement. Meters enforced on weekends and holidays.

east_middle_beach.boundary: 23rd Street to 44th Street, Collins Avenue to Indian Creek

west_middle_beach.boundary: 23rd Street to 63rd Street (excluding East Middle zone)

north_beach.enforcement_hours: 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM, 7 days/week (some sources: 8 AM - 11 PM)
north_beach.notes: Cheapest zone. Free parking at 500 14th St (up to 12 hours). New residential parking permit pilot program (Zone 26) launched Nov 2024.

resident_parking_discount.rate: $1.00/hour at on-street, garages, and surface lots
resident_parking_discount.registration: Must register with Resident Parking Discount Program

residential_permit_zones.enforcement: Varies by zone - check posted signage. Some zones say 'INCLUDING HOLIDAYS' meaning enforcement never stops.
residential_permit_zones.south_beach_zones: Multiple RPZ zones throughout South Beach, particularly in residential blocks west of Collins Ave and near West Avenue, Flamingo Park area

north_beach_pilot.name: North Beach Registered Resident Parking Pilot Program (Zone 26)
north_beach_pilot.eligibility: Must live within designated North Beach Zone with proof of residence

coral_gables.managed_by: City of Coral Gables Parking and Mobility Services
coral_gables.address: 254 Minorca Ave, Suite 204, Coral Gables, FL 33134

monthly_permits.purchase_rule: Must buy quarterly. Cannot purchase in last 5 business days of month.

coral_gables.validation_tickets: $4.00 off parking rate. Sold in sheets of 8. Request 3 business days in advance.
coral_gables.shops_at_merrick_park: Validated parking via Equinox or Nordstrom

rush_hour_restrictions.rule: No closures, lane reductions, or traffic holds during rush hour: Monday-Friday 7:00 AM - 10:00 AM and 3:30 PM - 7:00 PM
rush_hour_restrictions.applies_to: Special events, construction, road work on major arterials

special_event_zones.kaseya_center_area: Temporary tow-away zones activated on event nights around NE 8th St / Biscayne Blvd. Signs posted in advance. Enforcement is aggressive on event nights.
special_event_zones.general: Tow-Away Zone and No Parking signs include hours and days. Event nights frequently overlap with restricted windows.
special_event_zones.biscayne_blvd: Major north-south arterial near arena district. Temporary restrictions common during major events.

city_of_miami.permanent_tow_zones: Fire lanes, fire hydrants (must be 10+ feet away), crosswalks, sidewalks, bicycle lanes, double-parking, blocking driveways, handicap spaces (without valid placard)
city_of_miami.towing_enforcement: 24 hours/day, 7 days/week for illegal parking violations

city_of_miami_beach.general_tow_zones: Fire lanes, fire hydrant zones, crosswalks, sidewalks, bicycle lanes, restricted residential areas, disabled parking spaces, double-parking, blocking driveways/vehicles
city_of_miami_beach.payment_at_tow_lot: CASH ONLY. ATMs available on-site. Credit cards NOT accepted.
city_of_miami_beach.storage_fees: Accumulate daily. The longer the car remains, the more you pay.
city_of_miami_beach.drop_fee: FL law allows up to 50% of posted rate, but Miami Beach towing companies waive drop fees for public policy tows only.

parking_changes: Collins Avenue (5th St to Espanola Way): No parking starting 5 AM during spring break weekends
Washington Avenue (5th-16th St): No parking starting 5 AM during spring break weekends
100 blocks of 6th-15th Streets: No parking 24 hours/day during spring break weekends
spring_break_special_rules.enhanced_enforcement: License plate readers, drones, video technology. Priority enforcement in residential areas.
spring_break_special_rules.notes: Miami Beach actively discourages spring break. Curfew may be imposed. DUI checkpoints set up.

holiday_courtesy_program.name: Holiday Parking Courtesy Citation Program
holiday_courtesy_program.typical_dates: Mid-December through January 1 (e.g., Dec 17 12:01 AM - Jan 1 11:59 PM)
holiday_courtesy_program.how_it_works: Customers pay for parking normally via PayByPhone or ParkMobile. When session expires, instead of a real citation, they receive a holiday courtesy citation flyer granting up to 1 additional hour beyond the paid session.
holiday_courtesy_program.applies_to: All on-street parking spaces managed by Miami Parking Authority
holiday_courtesy_program.schedule: Every day, 24 hours/day during the program period
holiday_courtesy_program.important: This is NOT free parking. You still must pay initially. You get 1 free overtime hour only.

regular_holidays.note: The City of Miami / MPA does NOT publish a list of holidays where meters are fully suspended. Enforcement is generally active 7 days/week including most holidays. The holiday courtesy program is the primary holiday benefit.

city_of_miami_beach.holiday_enforcement: Miami Beach enforces parking meters on ALL days including Sundays and holidays. There is NO blanket holiday suspension of meters.
city_of_miami_beach.important_signage_rule: Some residential permit zones are posted 'INCLUDING HOLIDAYS' - these zones are enforced year-round with no exceptions.
city_of_miami_beach.holiday_impact: City Hall closes on observed holidays. Parking ENFORCEMENT continues. Meters remain active. The only change is City Hall administrative offices are closed for citation disputes/payments.
city_of_miami_beach.holiday_courtesy: Similar to City of Miami, Miami Beach has occasionally offered holiday courtesy citation programs during late December, but this is not guaranteed annually.

coral_gables.note: Coral Gables office hours are Mon-Fri 8 AM - 4:30 PM. Holiday enforcement details not published; likely follows standard 9 AM - midnight enforcement including most holidays.

meter_exemption.description: Vehicles displaying a valid disabled parking permit or disabled specialty license plate may park for FREE at any on-street metered space for up to 4 hours.
meter_exemption.notes: The disabled patron does NOT need to activate a parking session at a pay station or through a mobile app.

requirements: Must be issued to the person being transported
The vehicle must be transporting the person with the disability
does_NOT_override: Local time restrictions shorter than 4 hours
florida_state_law.misuse_penalties: Fines and possible permit revocation for using another person's placard

miami_specific.city_of_miami: Free accessible parking at all MPA meters, lots, and garages for vehicles with valid disabled permits
miami_specific.miami_beach: Same Florida state law applies. Free parking at on-street meters for up to 4 hours with valid placard.

miami_dade_county.note: Miami-Dade County sets the base fine schedule. Cities within the county (Miami, Miami Beach, etc.) follow this schedule with local additions.
miami_dade_county.court_hearing: May impose additional fines up to $100 plus court costs
miami_dade_county.collections: Citations unpaid for 90+ days reported to outside collection/credit reporting agency

general_rules: Park no more than 18 inches from curb/sidewalk
No parking on sidewalks, crosswalks, or alleys
Park head-on only (no backing in unless marked)
Only park where curbs are unpainted

downtown_miami.street_sweeping: No posted no-parking restrictions for sweeping
key_notes: MPA garages offer first 30 minutes free
Whole Foods (299 SE 3rd Ave) offers free customer parking
Miami-Dade Cultural Center Garage: $3/half hour
Bayfront Park area: $20 flat rate common
Limited metered street parking supply
Aggressive towing on event nights near Kaseya Center
downtown_miami.tow_away: Temporary tow zones near Kaseya Center on event nights. Rush hour restrictions Mon-Fri 7-10 AM, 3:30-7 PM.

brickell.street_sweeping: No posted no-parking restrictions for sweeping
key_notes: Very limited on-street metered parking
Brickell City Centre: first 2 hours free (no validation needed)
Mary Brickell Village: 2 hours free with Publix validation
620 Brickell: premium public parking garage
Most parking is in private building garages
Monthly garage parking: ~$250/month

wynwood.street_sweeping: No posted no-parking restrictions for sweeping
key_notes: Private lot rates spike dramatically on weekends and during events ($4-40+)
Employee parking: $30/month with decal (~260 spaces)
Monthly resident parking: $55/month (~85 spaces)
Wynwood Garage available for overflow
Art Basel and Wynwood Walls events cause extreme parking demand
Multiple private lots at NW 23rd/NW 5th and NW 2nd behind convention center

south_beach.street_sweeping: Commercial corridors swept overnight; no posted parking restrictions for sweeping in entertainment district
key_notes: Most expensive parking in metro Miami
19 residential permit zones throughout
Garages: 7th St, 12th St, 13th St garages at $1-2/hour
17th Street Garage: $2/hour, max $20/day
Fifth & Alton Garage: $2/hour, max $20/day
1111 Lincoln Road: $30/hour (architectural landmark garage)
Non-resident towing: $516 in South Beach (double rate)
Municipal lots do NOT allow overnight parking
Spring break: extreme enforcement, flat-rate garages, additional restrictions

coconut_grove.street_sweeping: No posted no-parking restrictions for sweeping
key_notes: Meters free until 10 AM - one of few Miami neighborhoods with this benefit
Sushi Maki: free parking for up to 2 hours
Time limits: 2-4 hours on most metered spaces
Public facilities managed by MPA
Meter space rental requires permit from Coconut Grove NET office

coral_gables.parking_authority: Coral Gables Parking and Mobility Services
coral_gables.street_sweeping: No posted no-parking restrictions for sweeping on main roads
key_notes: Miracle Mile and downtown area: $3.50/hour meters
Shops at Merrick Park: validated parking via Equinox/Nordstrom
Residential permit: $20/year per vehicle
Visitor passes: 4 included per household, $5/day each additional
Monthly garage permits: $112-123/month
Validation tickets: $4 off rate, must request 3 business days ahead
Lost garage ticket: $18 charge
Trolley service available (free) to reduce parking demand

little_havana.street_sweeping: No posted no-parking restrictions for sweeping
key_notes: Calle Ocho (SW 8th St) between 13th-18th Ave: metered parking
Free parking: Lot 82 in Cultural District
Adjacent streets (SW 5th, SW 7th): free unrestricted parking
20% discount for City of Miami residents via Pay By Phone
Cross streets like 14th Avenue often easier to find spots

design_district.street_meters: Limited MPA-managed meters on surrounding streets
design_district.street_sweeping: No posted no-parking restrictions for sweeping
key_notes: 4 garages operated by Miami Design District
Payment via ParkWhiz app (contactless, license plate based)
10 valet stations with interchangeable drop-off/pick-up
Palm Court Garage: $3 for first 4 hours (best value)
Museum Garage: architectural landmark, closes at 10 PM

midtown.parking_authority: Miami Parking Authority (street) + Midtown Miami CDD (garages)
midtown.street_sweeping: No posted no-parking restrictions for sweeping
key_notes: Midtown Miami CDD operates Northblock and Southblock garages
Street meters managed by MPA
Monthly garage parking: ~$150/month

north_beach_miami_beach.street_sweeping: Active alternate-side program in designated areas, 9 AM - 12 PM on designated weekdays
key_notes: Cheapest parking zone in Miami Beach
Free parking at 500 14th St (up to 12 hours)
New Residential Parking Permit Pilot (Zone 26) launched Nov 2024
After 6 PM: no meter fees
Active street cleaning program with posted parking restrictions

city_of_miami.curb_distance: Park no more than 18 inches from sidewalk/curb
city_of_miami.crosswalks: No parking within 20 feet of crosswalk
city_of_miami.direction: Must park in direction of traffic flow
city_of_miami.time_limits: Observe posted time limits even at free spots
city_of_miami.residential_zones: Look for residential permit signs before parking

city_of_miami_beach.head_in_only: Must park head-on (no backing in unless specifically marked)
city_of_miami_beach.unpainted_curbs: Only park where curbs are unpainted
city_of_miami_beach.residential_signs: Check for meters/machines before parking in residential areas
city_of_miami_beach.no_overnight_municipal_lots: Municipal lots do NOT allow overnight parking
city_of_miami_beach.holidays: Enforcement active on ALL days including holidays
city_of_miami_beach.towing_for_meters: Generally NOT towed for unpaid meters if parked legally - tickets only. Towing is for illegal parking (fire lanes, handicap, residential zones, etc.)

key_differences_miami_vs_miami_beach.enforcement_aggressiveness: Miami Beach is significantly more aggressive than City of Miami in parking enforcement, especially in South Beach
key_differences_miami_vs_miami_beach.rates: Miami Beach South Beach ($4/hr) is 2-3x more expensive than most City of Miami meters ($1-2/hr)
key_differences_miami_vs_miami_beach.hours: Miami Beach South Beach enforces until 3 AM; City of Miami typically enforces until midnight
key_differences_miami_vs_miami_beach.sundays_holidays: Both enforce 7 days/week including holidays. Neither offers blanket Sunday free parking.
key_differences_miami_vs_miami_beach.towing_cost: Miami Beach non-resident towing ($258-516) is far more expensive than typical Miami towing
key_differences_miami_vs_miami_beach.street_sweeping: Neither city has an aggressive NYC/SF-style ticketing program for street sweeping. Miami Beach has limited programs in Flamingo Park (delayed) and North Beach.
key_differences_miami_vs_miami_beach.resident_discounts: City of Miami: 50% off via MPA apps. Miami Beach: flat $1/hr for registered residents.
key_differences_miami_vs_miami_beach.payment_apps: City of Miami: PayByPhone, ParkMobile, Passport. Miami Beach: ParkMobile.
